Bug audit: sidebar and project management
────────────────────────────────────────
ULID:      01KKAWM4Y4E24TFEAPSDXZS99G
Slugs:     bug-sidebar
Type:      bug
Status:    pending_review
Priority:  3
Automation: eligible
Session:   01KKB2CRQGH3D0RA639KEWWP10

─── Description ───
  Investigate and fix bugs in sidebar UI. Check: drag-to-reorder losing position, inline rename not saving on blur, context menu positioning at screen edges, category collapse state, project color picker, close-all-tabs not cleaning tile layout, session count badge.
Spec ref:  @sidebar
Created:   2026-03-10T03:28:36.292Z
Started:   2026-03-10T05:14:09.647Z

─── Spec Context ───
Sidebar Navigation
Type: feature
Implementation: in_progress
Description:
  Project tree with categories, session lists, inline rename, drag-to-reorder, context menus. Desktop: collapsible left panel with resize handle. Mobile: drawer overlay with hamburger toggle.

─── Notes ───
[2026-03-10T05:24:52.338Z] @claude:
4 bugs fixed:
1. useDragAndDrop.ts: stale dropTarget position — handleProjectDrop and handleCategoryHeaderDrop read dropTarget?.position from React state closure which could be stale when drop fires (React may not have re-rendered since last dragOver). Added dropTargetRef that's updated synchronously alongside state, so drop handlers always read the latest position.
2. useSidebarHandlers.ts: rename Escape saves via blur — pressing Escape during inline rename calls setEditingProject(null), React unmounts the input, browser fires blur with the OLD handler that still has editingProject in closure, causing an unwanted save. Added renameCancelledRef that gets set on Escape and checked in the blur handler.
3. useSidebarHandlers.ts: same Escape→blur bug for category rename — identical pattern, added categoryRenameCancelledRef.
4. useProjectHandlers.ts: handleCloseProjectTabs missing undo history — unlike handleCloseTab (single tab), closing all project tabs didn't save to closedTabsRef, making undo impossible. Added push to closedTabsRef for each closed tab.

Operations on individual tasks

Options:
  -h, --help                      display help for command

Commands:
  list [options]                  List all tasks (alias for 'kspec tasks list')
  get [options] <ref>             Get task details
  add [options]                   Create a new task
  set [options] [ref]             Update task fields
  patch [options] <ref>           Update task with JSON data
  start [options] <ref>           Start working on a task (pending|needs_work

  delete [options] [ref]          Delete a task permanently

toolResponse.stdout:
Usage: kspec task complete [options] [ref]

Complete a task (pending_review -> completed)

Options:
  --refs <refs...>   Complete multiple tasks by ref
  --reason <reason>  Completion reason/notes
  --skip-review      Skip review requirement (requires --reason)
  --force            Force completion from any state (bypasses submit
                     requirement)
  --no-sync          Skip syncing spec implementation status
  -h, --help         display help for command

Examples:
  $ kspec task complete @task-slug --reason "Merged in PR #123"
  $ kspec task complete @task-slug --force --reason "Design task, no code to review"
  $ kspec task complete --refs @task1 @task2 --reason "Batch completion"
